observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive:

Require the designated Property Officer and Municipal Accountant to facilitate the reconciliation of the property accounts. Adjustments shall be made in the books or in the property records for variances noted or for proper disposition of properties; and

Require the designated Property Officer to prepare and maintain the required property records and reports for all PPE accounts.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct:

The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to validate their records, reconcile any identified reconciling items and effect the necessary adjustments in order to present a reliable balance of the CIB-LCCA account in the financial statements; and 

The Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain subsidiary ledger for all bank accounts maintained by the Municipality.

We recommended and Management agreed that:

The Management enforce strictly the terms and conditions of the lease contract to give way to other prospective lessees who have the capacity to pay the monthly stall rental fees; 

The Municipal Treasurer strengthen the collection system of market stall rentals by imposing sanctions to delinquent lessees in order to sustain the revenue sources of the Municipality. Exert more effort in collecting the accumulated delinquent rental fees; and

The Municipal Accountant record in the books the uncollected market stall rental fees based on the list to be provided by the Municipal Treasurer. Henceforth, recognize income using the accrual basis of accounting and record the corresponding receivable account in conformity with the prescribed standards.

We recommended and Management agreed to update its Municipal Revenue Code pursuant to the provisions of RA No. 7160 in order to maximize its revenue raising power and sustain the governmental operations of the Municipality.

We recommended and Management agreed that the BAC Secretariat utilize the PhilGEPS website and that of the Municipality by posting the necessary Notice of Award, Notice to Proceed and approved contract for each infrastructure project to promote transparency in government procurement.

We recommended that the Management exhaust all possible remedies to expedite liquidation of the cash advances and be guided with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 in case a request for write-off is prepared for account balances considered as dormant.

We recommended that the Management require:

The Inventory Committee to conduct the semestral physical count of inventories, prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories and submit the same to the Auditor;

The Designated Supply Officer and Municipal Accountant to prepare and update the stock cards and supplies ledger cards, respectively, for each inventory item. The Designated Supply Officer also prepare the SSMI which will be the basis of the Municipal Accountant for recording in the books of all issuances made. 

Periodic reconciliation of accounting records and records maintained by the Designated Supply Officer shall be undertaken so that discrepancies shall be immediately verified and adjusted. 

Investigate the non-moving/non-existing inventories then make proper adjustments thereof to reflect the true balances of the inventory account.
We recommended that projects, programs and activities be individually specified and not lumped into one project so that prompt review of these transactions can be done.
We recommended that the GAD Focal Person maintain a record of GAD related expenses to facilitate review and evaluation of the GAD expenses.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record back the remaining infrastructure assets costing ₱39.847 million dropped from the books in CY 2016 at their depreciated replacement cost or fair market value. Support the determination of costs with working paper. Provision for depreciation and impairment for these infrastructure assets shall follow the same procedures as other PPE items.
We strongly reiterated that the defaulting accountable officer produce immediately the undeposited collections and/or submit a written explanation why the collections remained undeposited.

We strongly reiterated our recommendation to create a Local Solid Waste Management Fund to which funds for the SWM program, which include the Municipality’s own appropriations, collections of SWM fees and fines, grants and donations, shares from public-private partnerships and incentives, be pooled.

We recommended the following:

The Municipal Treasurer issue warrants of levy and conduct public sale or auction of delinquent real properties. These are indispensable actions to avoid the criminal and administrative penalties provided in RA No. 7160. Coordinate with other local treasurers who had recently conducted public sale or auction in their own local government units to adopt their procedures and best practices.

Management provide support to the Office of the Municipal Treasurer in the conduct of public sale or auction. Include in the Municipality’s appropriations the budgetary requirements of the activity and provide augmentation of personnel when needed.

We recommended that the Property Officer – Designate and the Municipal Accountant reconcile their records on land owned by the Municipality. Management exhaust means to obtain records and documents from where acquisition costs of land be based. The Municipal Accountant record land at acquisition cost as prescribed in PAG 2, PPSAS 17.

We recommended that collections derived from tourism activities be utilized for tourism investments, projects and activities. Investment planning for tourism development and promotion projects and activities be supported with actual appropriations. We reiterate our prior year’s recommendation to create a separate Municipal Tourism Office with its own appropriations and appoint a full-time Municipal Tourism Officer.

We recommended the following:

All assets and liabilities, and revenues and expenses of the VAJMCC accrue to and be recorded in the Municipality’s General Fund books; and

For monitoring purpose, the Municipal Accountant at the end of each year prepare a Statement of Financial Position and Statement of Financial Performance for the VAJMCC.

1.10  We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct:
The Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical count of PPEs and coordinate with other concerned offices in locating the documents and whereabouts of the properties and equipment of the Municipality;
The designated Property Officer and Municipal Accountant to continuously work together in reconciling their records. Necessary adjustments shall be made in the books or in the property records for discrepancies noted; and
The designated Property Officer to prepare and maintain Property Cards for all PPE accounts as a means to record the receipt and issuance/transfer of PPE.

2.8	We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive as the Chairman of the Inventory Committee direct:
The members of the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of inventories accurately and completely to fairly present the Inventory account;
The Designated Supply Officer and Municipal Accountant to conduct quarterly reconciliation of the Inventory account to ensure that the balance of the said account on their separate records is reconciled; and
The Inventory Committee to coordinate with other concerned offices in locating the documents and/or physical existence of the dormant accounts so that adjustments could be made in the books and supply records once identified and supported by sufficient documents.

3.8	We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare journal entry vouchers to correct and adjust the accounts Loans Receivable – GOCCs and Loans Payable – Domestic in order to present a more reliable balances in the financial statements.

4.6	We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer to coordinate, identify and locate the necessary supporting documents for the prior year’s projects and effect adjustments in the books in order to reflect the correct balance of the CIP accounts in the financial statements.

5.11	We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct:
The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer together with other Accountable Officers to coordinate at least quarterly to identify the difference in amount and effect adjustments in the cash – local treasury account for fair presentation in the financial statements; and
The Municipal Treasurer to record daily collections in the cashbook as well as deposits made during the day for proper accountability.

6.10	We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify and present the amount under the non-current portion of the financial liabilities to fairly present the account in the Financial Statements and in compliance with the provisions of IPSAS 1.

7.8	We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to present the Financial Expenses separately from MOOE in the Financial Statements to reflect the correct balances of both line items.
We recommended and Management agreed to ensure in the succeeding years that transfer of actual cash pertaining to unexpended balance of current year DRRM be made immediately at year-end.

We recommended and Management agreed to prepare and submit to the Audit Team the Monthly Report of Official Travel, together with the original copy of a serially numbered Driver’s Trip Tickets. Also, a Report of Fuel Consumption be prepared and submitted showing the required details in accordance with COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, based on the certified list, direct the Municipal Accountant to take up and adjust in the books the RPT/SET receivables, with a corresponding subsidiary ledger that will monitor the collections and delinquencies in order to present a more reliable balance in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make a review of the accounts to determine the cause of the unreconciled amounts. Furthermore, prepare a reconciliation of the two reciprocal accounts and effect the necessary adjusting entries to bring the balances of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts into agreement in order to establish the accuracy, reliability and fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended the following:

The Municipal Administrator take over the duty of countersigning checks in settlement of the Municipality’s obligations from the Municipal Mayor.

The Municipal Treasurer assist the Municipal Administrator in making representation with the Municipality’s depository bank that he is one of the official signatories in the drawing of the Municipality’s checks and in the taking of his specimen signatures.

The next in rank second-level permanent staff/employee in the Office of the Municipal Administrator take over the countersigning of checks during the Municipal Administrator’s temporary absence or incapacity.
We recommended to require the Binirayan Foundation, Inc. to immediately submit its FUR because the time to liquidate has already elapsed.

We recommended that the Municipality’s garbage trucks collect only non-recyclable/non-recoverable materials in the MRFs of the barangays for transport to the residual containment area (RCA). Require the public market, schools, business other establishments to likewise establish and maintain their MRFs.

We recommended the following:

The Management exhaust all possible remedies to expedite liquidation of the cash advances and be guided with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 in case a request for write-off is prepared for account balances considered as dormant; and

The Municipal Accountant analyze the details of the negative balances and effect the necessary adjustments.

Notices to payees who have custody of checks, which will become stale, shall be sent by the Municipal Treasurer at least one month before the same become stale to inform them of the status thereof.

Record all her collections daily in her cashbook including all amounts turned over to her by the Collecting Officer to reflect the correct balances of the collections, and deposit intact all collections made during the day; and
Submit monthly to the Audit Team the Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF).

We recommended that the Management strictly monitor the completeness of information contained in the signboards, to encourage public participation in the monitoring of implementation of the various projects of the Municipality, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

We recommended that the Management:

Return to the National Treasury any unutilized balance and the total amount of ₱8,620.00, which is not among the allowable expenditures using BGCM, after the lifting of state of calamity.

Prospectively, ensure that the required documents for emergency procurements are complete before payments are made as required by GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 dated April 6, 2020.

We recommended that the Management:

Monitor the implementation of GAD related PPAs as well as the expenditures disbursed and check if it is still within the allocated amount in the GPB.

Enhance the formulation of the GPB through adequate planning so as to include all GAD related PPAs. Also, improve the preparation of GAD Accomplishment Report by completely filling out the GAD activity and corresponding outcomes or accomplishments.

We reiterated the following recommendations:

For road networks, comply with the accounting guidelines provided in COA Circular No. 2015-008. 



Prospectively, the Municipal Accountant record projects constructed and/or procured out of the Trust Fund as PPE when they qualify to the definition and recognition requirements of assets and PPE as provided in IPSASs 1 and 17, and recognize the proper revenues accordingly.

We recommended the following:

The Municipal Accountant record back the remaining infrastructure assets costing ₱39.847 million dropped from the books in CY 2016 at their depreciated replacement cost or fair market value. Support the determination of costs with working paper. Provision for depreciation and impairment for these infrastructure assets shall follow the same procedures as other PPE items.

We strongly reiterated the following recommendations:

The defaulting accountable officer produce immediately the undeposited collections and/or submit a written explanation why the collections remained undeposited.

The Municipal Mayor designate other permanent employees of the Municipality as collecting officers and be properly bonded to assist the Municipal Treasurer’s Office in its collecting function. Cash collections should not be assigned to job order personnel.

We strongly reiterated the following recommendations:

The MENRO – Designate follow-up the approval of the 10-year Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan with the National Solid Waste Management Commission through the Environmental Management Bureau Regional Office.

Create a Local Solid Waste Management Fund to which funds for the SWM program, which include the Municipality’s own appropriations, collections of SWM fees and fines, grants and donations, shares from public-private partnerships and incentives, be pooled.

We recommended the following:

The Municipal Treasurer exert more effort to collect delinquent real property taxes. Avail of all collection remedies applicable in the current circumstance of the Municipality.



The Municipal Treasurer issue warrants of levy and conduct public sale or auction of delinquent real properties. These are indispensable actions to avoid the criminal and administrative penalties provided in RA No. 7160. Coordinate with other local treasurers who had recently conducted public sale or auction in their own local government units to adopt their procedures and best practices.

Management provide support to the Office of the Municipal Treasurer in the conduct of public sale or auction. Include in the Municipality’s appropriations the budgetary requirements of the activity and provide augmentation of personnel when needed.

We recommended the following:

Require the Binirayan Foundation, Inc. to immediately submit its FUR because the time to liquidate has already elapsed.

We recommended the following:

Maximize utilization of the LDRRMF for disaster-related projects and activities and fast-track their implementation following timetables set in the Local DRRM Investment Plan.

Re-program and/or update DRRM projects and activities to be funded out of the unutilized prior years’ LDRRMF every year to reflect the current DRRM needs of the Municipality. Synchronize and consolidate planning for and utilization of the unexpended prior years’ LDRRMF with that of the current year LDRRMF.

We recommended the following:

The Property Officer – Designate and the Municipal Accountant reconcile their records on land owned by the Municipality. Management exhaust means to obtain records and documents from where acquisition costs of land be based. The Municipal Accountant record land at acquisition cost as prescribed in PAG 2, PPSAS 17.

We recommended the following:

The Municipality’s garbage trucks collect only non-recyclable/non-recoverable materials in the MRFs of the barangays for transport to the residual containment area (RCA). Require the public market, schools, business other establishments to likewise establish and maintain their MRFs.

We recommended that collections derived from tourism activities be utilized for tourism investments, projects and activities. Investment planning for tourism development and promotion projects and activities be supported with actual appropriations. We reiterate our prior year’s recommendation to create a separate Municipal Tourism Office with its own appropriations and appoint a full-time Municipal Tourism Officer.

We recommended the following:

All assets and liabilities, and revenues and expenses of the VAJMCC accrue to and be recorded in the Municipality’s General Fund books. 


In budgeting, tuition fees and all other fees be recognized as sources of income, and the VAJMCC be provided with a separate line item/account-based expenditures budget similar to a department/office of the Municipality.

For monitoring purpose, the Municipal Accountant at the end of each year prepare a Statement of Financial Position and Statement of Financial Performance for the VAJMCC.

We reiterated our recommendation that the balances of amounts withheld be remitted timely to the concerned government agencies in accordance with regulations. Reconcile balances which were not supported with documents by securing billing statements and through confirmation with other government agencies if they have outstanding receivables from the Municipality.
We recommended that COA Resolution No. 2017-021 in the installment payment of disallowances on salaries and other personnel benefits be strictly followed.

We recommended that management prioritize creation and appointment of three statutory DRRM staff who will assist the DRRM Officer in administration and training, research and planning, and operations and warning.

We recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan prescribe in its internal rules of procedure rules governing work schedule or performance standards of SB members which will be the basis of the pro-rated grant of RATA to the officials.

We recommended that management immediately submit documents showing that the payments of additional honoraria by the Municipality to the MBOC, RCG and Support Staff were authorized and with legal basis. Otherwise, payments will be disallowed in audit and persons liable will be made to refund the amounts received.

PAPs on children be complete and inclusive, and to contain programs to assist children in need of special protection (CNSP) and intervention and diversion programs for children in conflict with the law (CICL) among others.

We recommended the following:

Prepare and submit for review and endorsement the GPB timely and in accordance with the schedule set in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Circular No. 2016-01.

We commended and congratulated the Municipality for having received the 2017 SGLG. The award is a testament that the Municipality is dedicated in strengthening its good governance mechanisms and practices. We recommended that the Municipality’s officials and employees continuously improve and sustain these good governance efforts.
We recommended that COA Resolution No. 2017-021 in the installment payment of disallowances on salaries and other personnel benefits be strictly followed.

We recommended that management monitor efficient collection of RPT and SET receivables. Collection targets and efficiency measures be based on actual receivables recorded in the books rather than on fixed percentage of previous year’s collections to maximize the income potential of real property taxation.

We recommended that management prioritize creation and appointment of three statutory DRRM staff who will assist the DRRM Officer in administration and training, research and planning, and operations and warning.

We commended the Municipality for effectively implementing the project on reduction of plastic bags usage under the solid waste management program. We recommended that separate appropriations for the MENRO be provided and a full-time MENR Officer, either a Department Head or OIC meantime that finances do not permit, be appointed to carry out all other environmental programs.

We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator set up and maintain a GAD database containing complete data requirements enumerated in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01. The GFPS TWG to use sex-disaggregated data and other GAD-related information in developing a gender-responsive GAD Plan and Budget.

We recommended the withholding of the salary/claims of concerned officials and employees and imposition of other sanctions to those who failed to liquidate their cash advances within the prescribed period.

We recommend that a review of the composition of the Construction in Progress accounts be made and cost of completed projects be transferred to appropriate asset account. For public infrastructures funded out of regular income, the Construction in Progress account is transferred to the Public Infrastructures account upon completion. At the end of the year, the latter account is closed to the Government Equity and the asset is recorded in the Registry of Public Infrastructure.

We recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan prescribe in its internal rules of procedure rules governing work schedule or performance standards of SB members which will be the basis of the pro-rated grant of RATA to the officials.

We recommended that management immediately submit documents showing that the payments of additional honoraria by the Municipality to the MBOC, RCG and Support Staff were authorized and with legal basis. Otherwise, payments will be disallowed in audit and persons liable will be made to refund the amounts received.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive assign the Municipal Assessor to process the titles of these properties in the name of the LGU and should be properly kept by the general services officer or the local treasurer.  Moreover, the Accounting Unit should reconcile records per books with the actual value of lots owned by the LGU.

The Inventory Committee conduct physical count of the Municipality’s PPE annually.
Prepare promptly the RPCPPE and submit to the Office of the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year.
The Property Officer – Designate maintain the prescribed property cards and reconcile it with the RPCPPE to establish the correct amount of the PPE in the financial statements.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant provide and record the depreciation of depreciable PPE in accordance with the guidelines set by PPSAS 17 – PPE and other relevant COA regulations in the computation of depreciation.

We recommended that Management establish strong cash management mechanisms and controls through the following:
a. 	The Municipal Treasurer require all accountable officers to maintain a cashbook and to record all their transactions daily, and to close, rule, and certify their accounts and transactions in the cashbook at the end of each month.
b.	The Municipal Treasurer require the accountable officers to submit their Report of Collections and Deposits (RCD) daily, and strictly verify the RCD prepared by the accountable officers, especially on the correctness of the beginning and ending balances of their cash accountabilities and the completeness of the recording of their accountabilities on accountable forms.
c.	The Municipal Treasurer adopt a system wherein collecting officers turn over all their collections intact daily to her first, for verification of the correctness and completeness of the money turned over and its accompanying RCD. The Municipal Treasurer will then be the one who will deposit the collections to the bank.
d. 	The Municipal Treasurer require the accountable officers to prepare the Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) for consolidation and monitoring, and to firmly impose remittance of collections out of the issued accountable forms within the day.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit monthly BRSs pertaining to SEF and TF – Proper bank accounts. To fast-track completion and submission, designate other personnel under the Office of the Municipal Accountant to assist and augment in the preparation of the unsubmitted monthly BRSs.

a. 	The Municipality timely implement projects funded by the 20% Development Fund.

We recommend that management exert effort to locate the addresses of persons liable and demand the immediate settlement of audit disallowances. 


For those personnel who are still in service, immediate settlement of disallowance by directing the Municipal Treasurer to withhold payment of the salary and other emoluments to the satisfaction of the amounts disallowed.

We recommend that the LGU open a separate bank depository account with any of the authorized government depository banks for the 20% Development Fund. Once a separate depository account is opened, the Municipal Treasurer transfer the remaining balance of the 20% Development Fund to this account.

We recommend that management should strictly comply with the submission of contracts/purchase orders and all documents forming part thereof to the Auditor, within five (5) working days from execution to facilitate review processes, generate timely and relevant audit results and avoid enforcement of sanctions prescribed by law.

We recommend that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Journal Entry Voucher to record the reconciling items in order to present the correct balance of the Cash in Bank account of the General Fund.

We recommend that a review of the composition of the Construction in Progress accounts be made and cost of completed projects be transferred to appropriate asset account. For public infrastructures funded out of regular income, the Construction in Progress account is transferred to the Public Infrastructures account upon completion. At the end of the year, the latter account is closed to the Government Equity and the asset is recorded in the Registry of Public Infrastructure.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer be required to prepare the Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (CRAAF) and to submit the same together with the individual RAAF of all collectors in accordance with Section 50 and 51 of the NGAS Manual, Volume II. 


We also recommend that every time collectors will remit their daily collections, the Liquidating Officer/Municipal Treasurer shall require the presentation of the current and unused stubs to validate the last Official Receipt issued as appearing in the Report of Collection and Deposits and to sign the last Official Receipt issued.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer should strictly require all collecting clerks to remit their collections, as well as their accountable forms, at the close of each business day. The Treasurer/Liquidating Officer should strictly comply with Section 32 of NGAS-LGU to prevent any untoward incident to happen while so much cash is kept in the vault of the Treasurer’s Office. Compliance with the provisions of Sections 29 and 32 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for LGUs, Volume 1, should be strictly enforced, to protect government funds from risk of loss and/or misapplication.
